text:
There was a quiet town called Serenity Creek that had a problem with sirens. That’s right—every. Single. Day. The townfolks whispered about the cause. One too many late-night parties? An overenthusiastic fire department? No. Something a bit more, ahem, outlandish.

Little did they know, their secret lay in a single elderly man known only as Mr. Whiskers. The man loved his cats. Many of them. It was no surprise when he started constructing miniature rubber planes out of yarn. His hobbies were oddly specific, but to outsiders, they were just eccentric.

Until one fateful twilight when Mr. Whiskers was entangled in his latest design—a sleek racer—that snagged on a stray wire from Mr. Jenkins’s outdated alarm system. The wire cut deep into poor Mr. Whiskers's whiskers. With an agonizing yawn, he lost consciousness. That night, the sirens ceased.
